response.justification: Candidate is a seasoned senior engineer with strong full-stack credentials, DLang expertise (which suggests systems-programming aptitude relevant to C++/Rust thinking), and meaningful geospatial experience through GISCollective and Green Map. However, the role explicitly requires modern C++ or Rust as a primary skill, and the resume shows only a brief stint with C/C++ at National Instruments over a decade ago (2014-2015) with no recent commercial C++ or Rust work. No demonstrated experience with routing pipelines, map tiling, or large-scale distributed navigation systems. Worth a conversation given the geospatial background and systems-level DLang work, but the C++/Rust gap is significant.
strengths: Genuine geospatial/GIS domain experience via GISCollective and Green Map
Low-latency, high-throughput systems work at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic (millions of req/sec, async I/O, fibers)
Strong engineering discipline: TDD, CI/CD, testing tools authorship (Trial, Fluent-Asserts)
concerns: No recent modern C++ experience; only ~6 months of C/C++ in 2014-2015
No Rust experience listed anywhere on the resume
No exposure to routing tiles, navigation pipelines, or map data transformation at scale
